meta.intent: Make vine the future-action tax on an otherwise readable rescue route, so ignoring it feels like borrowing trouble rather than engaging a side mechanic.
meta.expectedPath: Clip the authored vine lane twice early to keep the shortcut open, rescue the lower urgent puppy, and only then pivot up to the safer target.
meta.expectedFailMode: The player lets the vine lane grow shut, spends extra actions rerouting, and then loses to combined water and dock pressure rather than one clean mistake.
meta.whatItProves: Vine supports the rescue-order seed by taxing future actions instead of stealing the board's identity.
meta.notes: The rebuilt board keeps the authored vine growth lane visually isolated, while adding enough surrounding material that clipping vine competes with real dock and rescue choices.
